title: Deterministic motion-cap + orientation-pin preset (4.7x better than accepted)
summary: On the bit-reproducible deterministic replay baseline (Stage A), the dominant ~0.67x metric-scale compression was traced to PER-STEP MOTION CAPS clipping fast segments (not an LIO scale-observability blocker as earlier weight-sweeps wrongly concluded). Loosening the caps recovered scale; strengthening the lidar point-to-plane factor + multihop relative pose factors then suppressed the residual rotation/terminal drift. Result: deterministic translation RMSE 0.284 m vs the cocolic_livo reference, down from the accepted production preset's 1.346 m.

preset_overrides_vs_accepted._note: All other params inherit the --enable-gaussian-map-feedback preset (contract=false legacy rendered_image mode). Pass weight flags as DOUBLE literals (64.0 not 64) or the node throws InvalidParameterTypeException.

result.smoothing_note: Optional continuous-time B-spline-style Gaussian position smoothing (sigma~4 samples) removes the small-amplitude high-freq wiggle: path 44.6m->33.1m (ref 33.35m), RMSE 0.2835->0.2816. Faithful to Coco-LIC's cumulative-B-spline trajectory form; legitimate output representation. Apply via scripts/loop_closure_refine.py-adjacent smoothing or post-process.
result.determinism: bit-identical across two runs, md5 f47896a0299bd4562f9fee39b161ead2 (raw, full 122s, coverage 0.954)

in_session_levers_exhausted_by_implement: loop closure (sensor-ICP + sparse SE3 PGO) -> worsens vs ref (trades end-fix for middle-degrade)
gravity-direction estimation -> worse (fixed gravity already correct)
continuous-time trajectory smoothing -> fixes path-drift, RMSE unchanged (wiggle is small-amplitude)
cm_path_multi_session_structural: time-varying bias spline + jointly-observable gravity to break accel/gravity ambiguity (trajectory_estimator.cpp:765-767,901-903)
true frame-to-MAP photometric vs in-loop GPU Gaussian render (not frozen-linearization CPU voxel-mean, continuous_time_node.cpp:2179-2185)
LiDAR promoted to tightly-coupled multi-knot continuous-time point-to-plane (CT interp currently visual-only, sliding_window_optimizer.cpp:2629-2648)

residual_analysis.structure: U-shaped: start (init/gravity-bias autocal transient) + terminal accumulated yaw drift; middle ~0.15-0.23 m
residual_analysis.config_floor: ~0.28-0.30 m; further scalar weight tuning is in the noisy/non-monotonic regime (sub-0.02 m differences are local-minimum noise, not signal)
residual_analysis.path_to_cm: structural: (1) better initialization to remove the start transient; (2) loop closure / global consistency for the terminal ~0.8 m drift (Coco-LIC/Gaussian-LIC2 is odometry; the reference closes the loop to 3.5 cm from odometry alone, so this is an odometry-faithfulness gap); (3) deeper tight-LIC coupling faithfulness.
